element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn efficiency through non-renewable resource management across early missions
Town Hall/Great Hall production; upgraded twice for efficiency gains
Unit production gates; farms used both as food and defensive walls
Melee and ranged units; prerequisite buildings required
Oil tankers for offshore platforms; combat ships for naval warfare
Offshore platforms require oil tanker delivery chain
Faction-specific advantages: Orcs strong in ground combat; Humans weaker
Humans possess more powerful spellcasters; faction-specific spell sets
Unlocks advanced units and upgrades; creates tech tree progression
Rescue missions, fort defense, escort objectives beyond standard destroy-opponent pattern

typical_relief_triggers: Successful resource collection enabling unit production
Completing tech tree unlocks and building prerequisites
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy unit advancement and tech progression
Food supply limits blocking army expansion
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating mid-game tension through resource scarcity and advancing tech; campaign missions alternate between standard destruction objectives and complex rescue/escort/defense scenarios to vary pacing

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Forests serve dual function as lumber resource and tactical barriers; players creatively use terrain for map control

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction to resource collection and worker management; basic unit production and economy
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate complexity increases with food supply gating, naval/oil mechanics, tech trees, and prerequisite building chains; mission objectives diversify
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dangerous escalation with advanced units, spellcasters, and aerial combat; faction-specific asymmetric balance (Orcs ground advantage, Humans naval/magic advantage)
notable_spikes: Mid-late game faction-specific unit asymmetry requiring strategic adaptation
Special objectives (rescue/escort) vs. standard destruction pattern

notable_level_patterns.description: Collect resources, build buildings and units, destroy all opponents. Foundation pattern for most campaign missions.
Objectives beyond simple destruction: rescue troops, defend forts, protect key units/characters. Adds tactical complexity beyond pure army building.
Guide important characters through enemy territory while maintaining army protection. Combines micro-management with macro strategy.
Missions leveraging shipyard production, oil tanker logistics, and naval warfare; maps with shoreline terrain and multiple resource islands.
Prerequisite building chains gate unit access; players unlock advanced units through building infrastructure rather than time/experience
